titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 5948

p.seg: To all Christ's faithful to whom the present writing shall have come, Robert son of Reginald of Great Reston sends greeting.

truly to have come to know, by many proofs, that I and my ancestors ought to have in the territory of the town of Great Riston but one ploughgate of land only, that is, one-hundred-and-five acres. Hence it is that I make known to you all that I have resigned, and have quitclaimed for myself and my heirs, and by rod and staff returned in the court of my lord, the prior of Coldingham, to the prior and monks of Durham at Coldingham serving God in that place now and for ever in the future, all the lands which I or my predecessors unjustly occupied and withheld above one ploughate of land in the territory of the toun of Great Reston; that is, in seven acres in Ilvingishope, eighteen-and-a-half acres in Langelandis, twelve-and-a-half acres in Mers, the whole land in Alaynescastel in return for two acres, two acres in Bilielawe, eight acres in the grassland which is called Swinwood, on the south side of the Eye [Water]; so that neither I nor my successors shall in the future be able to have any right or claim in the said lands. And this resignation and quitclaim I have made to the said monks for the salvation of my own soul and of all my ancestors' and successors' [souls].
And so that this resignation and quitclaim should have validity and strength I have applied my seal to the present writing.
